summary: Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to repel a Russian motorcycle assault in Zaporizhzhia, while elsewhere in the same region, FPVs struck two Russian soldiers on motorcycles. In a separate engagement, a Russian TOS-1A MLRS was first critically damaged by a Bulava loitering munition, then finished off by FPV UAVs. The Rubicon Center reported destroying 129 Ukrainian targets on April 18, including personnel and fixed-wing UAVs, and over 24,000 total enemy targets to date, with unmanned systems comprising 36.7% of destroyed targets. Ukrainian forces also captured a Ukrainian "Shark-M" UAV after its parachute deployed due to an intercept by the Russian "Yolka" air defense drone in the Krasny Liman area.
